channel.title: Activities on Mengtao (Tomy) Lyu｜吕孟韬
channel.description: Recent content in Activities on Mengtao (Tomy) Lyu｜吕孟韬

item.title: Paper Published (ADVEI)
item.description: Congrats to Zhimin!🎊 Our paper “Predicting detection failures in air traffic control: Leveraging fixation-state transitions and LLM-driven probabilistic threshold tuning ” was published at Advanced Engineering Informatics (ADVEI)

item.title: Two Papers Published (HCII 2026)
item.description: Congrats to Mercedes and Zhimin!!🎉 We got two papers published at HCII 2026 (23rd International Conference, EPCE 2026, Held as Part of the 28th HCI International Conference, HCII 2026, Montreal, QC, Canada) “Human-AI Decision Support for Sustainable Air Traffic Controller Workload: Forecast-Informed Bilevel-Optimized Task-Load Estimation ” from Mercedes; “How Multi-sector Planning Shapes Shared Situation Awareness Among Air Traffic Controllers: A Team-Level Eye-Tracking Evaluation ” from Zhimin;

item.title: ✈️Paper Published (ADVEI)
item.description: TRUST-UP: Trustworthy reinforcement learning using safe techniques for UAV pursuit In this study, we proposed a Trustworthy Reinforcement learning Using Safe Techniques for UAV Pursuit (TRUST-UP), which consists of two key components: a safety filter constructed from Control Barrier Functions (CBFs) that transforms unsafe RL actions into provably safe flight commands, and a switching strategy that enhances feasibility while maintaining operational transparency. These components enable trustworthy AI deployment in urban airspace, satisfying technical robustness and transparency requirements for aviation certification. Trustworthy Reinforcement-learning Using Safe Techniques for UAV Pursuit (TRUST-UP)

item.title: I have received the 2025/26 Outstanding Student Project Award
item.description: I’m delighted to share that I received the Outstanding Student Project Award, The Hong Kong Ergonomics Society (HKES) I woke up at 4:30 AM to attend the meeting online, while such milestones definately worth losing a little sleep 😄 (Also excited to share that I broke my benchpress PR that same morning, hitting 117.5kg in the gym, carrying that excitement😎) Award Certificate

item.title: Paper Published (CHI 2026)
item.description: Congrats to Yiteng! Our paper “From Expectation to Evaluation: Expectation Cues Systematically Bias LLM and Human Judgment ” was published at ACM CHI 2026 (ACM Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems, Barcelona, Spain)

item.title: Paper Published (IJHCS)
item.description: Congrats to Yiteng! Our paper “ARHUD design for dynamic spatial information presentation to improve driver situation awareness of blind spots” was accepted by International Journal of Human-Computer Studies

item.title: My publication has been featured in Tobii's 2025 Annual Research Report!
item.description: Inspiring, innovative and pioneering research in 2025 by Tobii My previous work Do you need help? Identifying and responding to pilots’ troubleshooting through eye-tracking and Large Language Model has been featured in Tobii’s 2025 Annual Research Report. I’m glad to see my works getting recognized, such little surprises in life keep motivating me 😁😁😁 Inspiring, innovative, and pioneering research in 2025 by Tobii

item.title: Come and join my talk on GenAI in Autonomous Driving HCI on 27 Feb!!
item.description: I’m excited to share some recent progress in my research at February HART TG Monthly Memebr Conversations I’ll be sharing my review work on GenAI techniques in Autonomous Driving HCIs through the lens of Situation Awareness (SA). This is such a rapidly evolving and fascinating area, and I hope my perspective can contribute some useful insights to the ongoing conversations. I’m sure there are important aspects I haven’t fully captured or considered. I’d love to hear your thoughts, questions, and different perspectives during and after the talk! Your feedback and insights would be incredibly valuable in helping me, and all of us, better understand these emerging technologies and their human-centered applications. Looking forward to a great discussion with everyone!🚗🤖 Zoom Meeting Meeting ID: 971 6234 9704 Passcode: HART26 Please join us!😆

item.title: I have received the 2025 IEA/Kingfar Student Award
item.description: I’m deeply honored and thrilled to share that I’ve received the 2025 IEA/Kingfar Student Award for Research in Human Factors and Ergonomics! This recognition means so much to me as it celebrates the work completed during my PhD journey. I’m energized to continue my research in Human Factors, Human-AI Teaming, and related areas, and I look forward to contributing to this vibrant research community. Award Certificate

item.title: I'm happy to share that I'm starting a new position as Postdoctoral Fellow at the HI Lab
item.description: I have joined the Hybrid Intelligence (HI) Lab directed by Mengyao Li on 15 Sep I’m thrilled to be part of a group dedicated to advancing human-AI collaboration and understanding how we can achieve better outcomes together than either humans or machines can alone. I look forward to this exciting new chapter in my academic journey.🚀 (Chinese cuisines here in Atlanta are amazing as these team fellows 😆) Say Hi to the HI Lab members

item.title: My paper has been accepted by International Journal of Human-Computer Studies
item.description: Do you need help? Identifying and responding to pilots’ troubleshooting through eye-tracking and Large Language Model In this study, we introduce an innovative approach that tokenizes eye-tracking data into Visual Attention Matrices (VAMs) and integrates them with Large Language Models to identify and respond to pilots’ troubleshooting activities in real-time. This represents one of the early works combining eye-tracking data with LLMs for adaptive aviation support systems. The method addresses two key challenges: capturing the complex troubleshooting behaviors of actively engaged (In-the-Loop) pilots, and effectively processing non-semantic eye-tracking data using LLM technology. Graphical Abstract

item.title: Farewell ATMRI, thanks for the wonderfull memories ❤️
item.description: I am deeply grateful to have met such a great team full of excellent brains and warm hearts As 5th Sep marks my final day at ATMRI, I wanted to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude to the team for making my time here so meaningful and rewarding. While I’m excited about the opportunities ahead, I will miss the time we spend together at ATMRI. 💕 The wonderful MSP team

item.title: I have officially graduated from The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU) on 10th May 2025
item.description: Feeling incredibly proud and grateful to have been able to complete my PhD at PolyU! I feel fortunate to have been able to work with such a talented team and to have been able to learn so much. Heartful thanks to my amazing supervisors, Dr. Fan Li and Dr. Gangyan Xu, for therir unwavering guidence and support. And a huge credit to my lovely teammates in the SHINE group, a terrific research team fulled with laughters and inspiring minds 🫶🫶 @Congregation | with President, Prof. Jin-Guang Teng

item.title: I have successfully defended my PhD theis on 23rd Jan 2025
item.description: Thanks to all the members of examaination board, and my friends came to support me! 2022.01.03～2025.01.23🎓 3 years & 20 days It was an exhilarating journey, filled with unexpected twists and frustrations Reaching a milestone, ready to move towards a more challenging and exciting future 💪🏻 PhD Defense
